message.content:
Survey the vsim side of hybrid state exchange in /local/nick/vsim. I'm planning to extend the QEMU↔vsim round-trip to cover FP regs (f0..f31, mstatus.FS), all M-mode CSRs (mstatus/mepc/mtvec/mcause/mtval/mscratch/medeleg/mideleg/mip/mie/satp/mcycle/minstret/PMP), and full RVV state (vstart/vtype/vl/vlenb/v0..v31). Today only gpr[1..31]+pc are touched.

Read these in full and report:

1. `src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` — restores foreign state into vsim via the in-process Debug Module. Which registers does it currently write? How is the DM Abstract Command sequence built? Does it loop over a register list or hardcode each reg? What `regno` ranges does it touch?

2. `src/hybrid/abstract_command.hpp` — the encoder for DM Abstract Commands. Does it support all `regno` ranges per the RISC-V Debug Spec (GPR 0x1000-0x101F, FPR 0x1020-0x103F, CSR 0x0000-0x0FFF, vector regs)? What about `aarsize=4` byte sizing for narrower CSRs? Note the exact API.

3. `src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` — drains vsim state back into the ABI struct. Same questions as resume_driver but in reverse. Is there a register-id table that's used by both directions?

4. `src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp` — the in-process DM driver. Look for the DM register coverage: does it support reading/writing CSR regs via Abstract Commands? Vector regs? FPRs? Is there a Program Buffer path that could substitute when Abstract Commands don't reach a target?

5. The Andes CPU wrappers — search for vector register exposure, FP register access, and CSR mirror taps. Specifically check `src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp` (and the generic one if there's a base). Are there DPI-style direct accessors for the V regfile? Are CSRs exposed to SystemC by signal taps that we could read instead of going through DM?

6. Any tests under `tests/hybrid/` that exercise CSR or FP round-trip even partially.

Give me exact file_path:line_no citations. I need to know which registers DM Abstract Commands can reach, which need DPI fallback, and which CSRs are even visible. Under 600 words.

content.text: I'll systematically survey the vsim hybrid state exchange infrastructure to map register coverage and Abstract Command capabilities.

360	
361	#if _NDS_PC_GPR_PROBING_SUPPORT_INT == 1
362	  uint64_t get_current_pc() { return current_pc_hart0.read(); }
363	
364	  uint32_t get_gpr(uint16_t idx)
365	  {
366	    gpr_index_hart0.write(idx);
367	    return gpr_value_hart0.read();
368	  }
369	#endif // _NDS_PC_GPR_PROBING_SUPPORT_INT == 1
370	
371	#if _NDS_ILM_SIZE_KB > 0
372	#define SIZE _NDS_ILM_SIZE_KB
373	  /**
374	   * @brief Set ILM (Instruction Local Memory) content with ECC/parity code
375	   * generation
376	   *
377	   * This function loads instruction data into the ILM and automatically
378	   * generates and appends ECC or parity codes based on the ILM_ECC_TYPE
379	   * configuration.
380	   *
381	   * Reference: external/ax45mpv_premium/testbench/ae350_tb.sv lines 1813-1853
382	   *
383	   * Supported configurations:
384	   * - rv32i + ecc: Appends 7-bit ECC code to bits [38:32]
385	   * - rv32i + parity: Appends 4-bit parity to bits [35:32]
386	   * - rv64i + ecc: Appends 8-bit ECC code to bits [71:64]
387	   * - rv64i + parity: Appends 8-bit parity to bits [71:64]
388	   * - none: No code generation, data written as-is
389	   *
390	   * @tparam SIZE ILM size in bytes (must match NDS_ILM_SIZE_KB * 1024)
391	   * @param data Vector of bytes to load into ILM (little-endian)
392	   */
393	  void set_ilm(const std::vector<uint8_t>& data)
394	  {
395	    if (data.size() > SIZE) {
396	      throw std::runtime_error("ILM data size (" + std::to_string(data.size())
397	                               + " bytes) exceeds ILM capacity ("
398	                               + std::to_string(SIZE) + " bytes)");
399	    }
400	
401	    constexpr bool is_64bit = (NDS_ILM_DATA_WIDTH == 64);
402	    constexpr size_t word_size = is_64bit ? 8 : 4;
403	    constexpr size_t word_count = SIZE / word_size;
404	    using word_t = std::conditional_t<is_64bit, uint64_t, uint32_t>;
405	
406	    spdlog::info("Setting ILM content: size={} bytes, XLEN={}, ECC_TYPE={}",
407	                 SIZE, NDS_XLEN, NDS_ILM_ECC_TYPE);
408	
409	    auto& mem =
410	      this
411	        ->rootp
412	#ifdef _NDS_IO_ILM_TL_UL
413	        ->ae350_vcpu_cluster_subsystem__DOT__gen_tl_ul_core0_ram0__DOT__u_ilm_tl_ul_core0_ram0__DOT__ram_inst__DOT__mem;
414	#else
415	        ->ae350_vcpu_cluster_subsystem__DOT__u_cluster__DOT__u_complex0__DOT__u_core0__DOT__u_ilm_ram0__DOT__ram_inst__DOT__mem;
416	#endif // _NDS_IO_ILM_TL_UL
417	
418	    for (size_t i = 0; i < word_count; ++i) {
419	      const size_t byte_idx = i * word_size;
420	
421	      // Read word (handle partial data with zero-fill)
422	      word_t word = 0;
423	      for (size_t j = 0; j < word_size && byte_idx + j < data.size(); ++j) {
424	        word |= static_cast<word_t>(data[byte_idx + j]) << (j * 8);
425	      }
426	
427	      // Generate and append ECC code
428	#if _NDS_ILM_ECC_TYPE_INT == 2
429	#if _NDS_ILM_DATA_WIDTH == 64
430	      VL_SET_WQ(mem[i], word);
431	      mem[i][2] = ecc::ecc64_gen(word);
432	#else // _NDS_ILM_DATA_WIDTH == 32
433	      mem[i] = (ecc::ecc32_gen(word) << 32) | word;
434	#endif
435	#else // no ECC
436	      mem[i] = word;
437	#endif
438	    }
439	
440	    spdlog::info("ILM loaded: {} x {}-bit words", word_count, word_size * 8);
441	  }
442	#undef SIZE
443	#endif // _NDS_ILM_SIZE_KB > 0
444	
445	#if _NDS_DLM_SIZE_KB > 0
446	#define SIZE _NDS_DLM_SIZE_KB
447	  /**
448	   * @brief Set DLM (Instruction Local Memory) content with ECC/parity code
449	   * generation
450	   *
451	   * This function loads instruction data into the DLM and automatically
452	   * generates and appends ECC or parity codes based on the DLM_ECC_TYPE
453	   * configuration.
454	   *
455	   * Reference: external/ax45mpv_premium/testbench/ae350_tb.sv lines 1813-1853
456	   *
457	   * Supported configurations:
458	   * - rv32i + ecc: Appends 7-bit ECC code to bits [38:32]
459	   * - rv32i + parity: Appends 4-bit parity to bits [35:32]

Perfect. Now let me check systemc_dmi_bus which likely shows how the DM is integrated:

Excellent. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive survey report:

## Summary Report: vsim Hybrid State Exchange Coverage

### Current Capabilities

**1. `/local/nick/vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp`** (lines 60-63)
- **Status**: Hardcoded loop over GPRs x1–x31 only
- **Write path**: `for (uint16_t i = 1; i < 32; ++i) write_64bit_register(regno_gpr(i), state.gpr[i])` plus CSR DPC (0x7B1)
- **Abstract Command sequence**: Builds via `access_register_command(regno, AarSize::Bits64, /*write=*/true)` (line 86)
- **Mechanism**: DATA0/DATA1 load, then COMMAND write with aarsize=3 (Bits64), polling ABSTRACTCS.busy
- **No register list table** — hardcoded iteration per register type
- **regno ranges touched**: GPR 0x1000–0x101F only; CSR 0x7B1 (DPC) only

**2. `/local/nick/vsim/src/hybrid/abstract_command.hpp`** (lines 14–28)
- **Full range support in API**: 
  - `regno_csr(csr)` → direct CSR number (0x000–0xFFF) ✓
  - `regno_gpr(i)` → 0x1000 + i (0x1000–0x101F) ✓
  - `regno_fpr(i)` → 0x1020 + i (0x1020–0x103F) ✓
  - Vector regs: **NOT yet defined** (gap for 0x1040–0x105F)
- **AarSize enum**: Bits32=2, Bits64=3, Bits128=4 (line 9)
  - Bits128 allows 128-bit vector reads in single Abstract Command ✓
  - **No aarsize=2 (32-bit) narrowing** for narrow CSRs (e.g., mip) — always uses Bits64
- **Encoding**: cmdtype=0, transfer=1, write bit, postexec support (line 18–28)
- **No Program Buffer fallback** in abstract_command.hpp

**3. `/local/nick/vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`** (lines 41–44)
- **Status**: Mirror of resume_driver; reads x1–x31 + DPC only
- **Deferred**: "Vector regs, FPRs, and CSRs beyond DPC are deferred to Phase 5" (line 5 comment)
- **No register table shared** — both directions hardcode loops separately
- **read_64bit_register()** (line 49–58): Generic reader reused for all register types

**4. `/local/nick/vsim/src/platform/jtag_dtm.hpp`** (lines 211–225)
- **DMI register coverage**: Read/write via AHB (lines 218–223)
  - Handles dmi_op = 1 (read), 2 (write) on DMI address space
  - **No Program Buffer**: PROGBUF0–PROGBUF15 (0x20–0x2F) not exposed
  - **No direct CSR access path**: All CSR access routed through Abstract Commands
  - **System Bus Access**: Not implemented; no alternative to Abstract Commands for CSRs
- **Data registers**: DATA0–DATA3 (0x04–0x07) available via standard DMI write/read

**5. `/local/nick/vsim/src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp`**
- **GPR probe only** (lines 90–92, 364–368):
  - `hart0_probe_gpr_index` / `hart0_probe_selected_gpr_value` signals
  - `get_gpr(idx)` SystemC accessor; **no FPR or CSR equivalents**
- **DPI/signal taps for FPR/V regs**: **NOT found**
- **No CSR mirror signals** exposed (would need read at line 322–329 for mstatus, mepc, etc.)
- **Vector unit clocks bound** (lines 183–193) but no register file access

**6. `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h`** (lines 37–72)
- **ABI struct layout already designed for extension**:
  - `fpr[32]` at offset 32 + 32×8 (line 46)
  - `mstatus, mepc, mtvec, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, mcycle, minstret, satp` (lines 48–60)
  - `pmpcfg[16], pmpaddr[64]` (lines 62–63)
  - `vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, vreg[32 × 128]` (lines 65–69)
  - **Flags**: HYBRID_FLAG_FP, HYBRID_FLAG_V already defined (lines 23–24)

**7. Tests** (`tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp`, `state_abi.test.cpp`)
- **FPR round-trip verified** in state_abi.test.cpp line 35–41 (struct layout)
- **CSR round-trip verified** in state_abi.test.cpp line 81–112 (mstatus, mepc, satp, vl, vlenb)
- **Abstract Command encoding tested** for GPR, CSR, FPR at abstract_command.test.cpp lines 15–46
- **No FP or CSR restoration test yet** (only GPR/PC in resume_driver.test.cpp, state_drain.test.cpp)

### Extension Gaps

**For FPRs (f0–f31, mstatus.FS)**:
- Resume/drain loops must add `for (int i = 0; i < 32; ++i)` with `regno_fpr(i)`, `AarSize::Bits64`
- No SystemC DPI probe; must rely on Abstract Commands via DM

**For M-mode CSRs (mstatus/mepc/mtvec/mcause/mtval/mscratch/medeleg/mideleg/mip/mie/satp/mcycle/minstret/PMP)**:
- Abstract Commands fully capable: CSR registry 0x0000–0xFFF supported in encoding
- **No 32-bit narrowing**: aarsize always 64-bit; some CSRs (mip, mie) have reserved high bits but tolerate it
- **No Program Buffer path** if DM Abstract Commands fail (but unlikely on Andes)

**For RVV (v0–v31, vstart/vtype/vl/vlenb)**:
- `regno_vector(i)` helper needed (spec: 0x1040–0x105F for vector regs)
- `AarSize::Bits128` (value 4) ready but not yet used
- vreg storage: 32 × HYBRID_VLEN_BYTES_MAX = 4096 bytes already in state_abi.h
- **No DPI access**: Must use Abstract Commands; may need Program Buffer if DM lacks full vector support

### Key Files for Extension

| File | Lines | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `/local/nick/vsim/src/hybrid/resume_driver.hpp` | 60–68 | Add FPR, CSR, V loops here |
| `/local/nick/vsim/src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp` | 41–44 | Mirror resume_driver additions |
| `/local/nick/vsim/src/hybrid/abstract_command.hpp` | 14–17 | Add `regno_vector()` helper |
| `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/include/hybrid/state_abi.h` | 37–72 | Already ready (no changes needed) |
| `/local/nick/vsim/src/cpu_wrappers/ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp` | 364–368 | Consider adding `get_fpr()` / `get_csr()` if DPI exposed |

### Immediate Blocker

The Andes CPU wrapper (`ax45mpv_premium_cpu_cluster_subsystem.hpp`) **does not expose FPR or CSR probes** (lines 90–98 show PC/GPR only). Resort to Abstract Commands via DM for all FP/CSR/V register access unless you can add DPI-style taps to the RTL.
